channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Rescuers dig for survivors of Vanuatu earthquake that killed 14 people and injured more than 200
item.description: WELLINGTON, New Zealand (AP) — Vanuatu's capital was without water on Wednesday, a day after reservoirs were destroyed by a violent magnitude 7.3 earthquake that wrought havoc on the South Pacific island nation, killing at least 14 people and injuring hundreds, with the figure expected to rise.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Verdicts are due in the historic French rape trial that turned Gisèle Pelicot into a feminist hero
item.description: French judges plan to deliver hugely anticipated verdicts this week in a historic drugging-and-rape trial that has turned the victim, Gisèle Pelicot, into a feminist hero.
item.dc:creator: John Leicester, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Israel's borders have shifted throughout its history. Action in Syria may reshape them again
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israel's prime minister, Benjamin Netanyahu, entered Syrian territory Tuesday and said Israeli troops would remain in the area indefinitely, blurring the border with its northern neighbor. Since its establishment in 1948, Israel has never had fully recognized borders.
item.dc:creator: JOSEF FEDERMAN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Filipina who won a last-minute reprieve from an Indonesian firing squad returns home
item.description: JAKARTA, Indonesia (AP) — A Filipina woman who had been on death row in Indonesia, and who was nearly executed by firing squad in 2015, arrived home early Wednesday after longstanding requests from her home country.
item.dc:creator: NINIEK KARMINI and TERESA CEROJANO, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Japanese space startup aborts 2nd satellite launch attempt minutes after liftoff
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — A Japanese space startup said its second attempt to launch a rocket carrying satellites into orbit had been aborted minutes after liftoff Wednesday, nine months after the company's first launch attempt ended in an explosion. Space One's Kairos No.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Canada's prime minister faces calls to resign. Here's what could happen next
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau faces growing pressure to resign from his own Liberal Party after his top minister abruptly quit and criticized his handling of the budget.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Middle East latest: Israel plans an extended occupation of Syrian buffer zone
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said Israeli troops will occupy a buffer zone inside Syria for the foreseeable future.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Deadly violence in Nigeria linked to breakup of United Methodist Church over LGBTQ policies
item.description: A religious schism has turned deadly in Nigeria, with a church member fatally shot and two young children killed as homes were set ablaze, according to United Methodist News Service.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The killing of a Russian general is one of several that Moscow blames on Ukraine
item.description: Ukraine’s Security Service, or SBU, has claimed responsibility for killing Lt. General Igor Kirillov, head of the military’s nuclear, biological and chemical protection forces, in a bombing Tuesday in Moscow.
item.dc:creator: KATIE MARIE DAVIES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 2 charged in deadly drone attack on US soldiers in Jordan
item.description: The U.S. on Monday charged two Iranian officials in a scheme that contributed to a deadly January drone attack that killed three American service members in Jordan. The Department of Justice (DOJ) charged Mahdi Mohammad Sadeghi, 42, who is a U.S.-Iranian national from Natick, Mass.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Netanyahu spokesperson denies Cairo trip amid Gaza ceasefire push
item.description: A spokesperson for Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Tuesday rejected reports the Israeli leader was traveling to Cairo for negotiations over a ceasefire and hostage release deal with Hamas in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: US Embassy seriously damaged in Vanuatu earthquake
item.description: A forceful 7.4 magnitude earthquake shook the capital of Vanuatu damaging the newly established U.S. Embassy on Tuesday. Authorities reported the earthquake and subsequent aftershocks at 12:53 p.m. local time, according to an embassy alert.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Authorities impose overnight curfew in cyclone-ravaged Mayotte as France rushes in aid
item.description: An overnight curfew goes into force Tuesday evening as Mayotte struggles to recover from Cyclone Chido, the strongest storm to hit the French Indian Ocean archipelago in 90 years.
item.dc:creator: Adrienne Surprenant And Tom Nouvian, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Battle erupts over South Korean court that will determine the fate of impeached Yoon
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea’s acting leader vowed Tuesday to convey to the world that things are back to normal following parliament’s impeachment of conservative President Yoon Suk Yeol, as rival parties squabble the appointments of court justices who will determine whether to unseat or r
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG and HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ukrainian official takes credit after Russian general Igor Kirillov killed by explosive device in Moscow
item.description: An official with the Ukrainian Security Services says the agency was behind the bombing assassination of Lt. Gen. Igor Kirillov, commander of Russia’s chemical, biological and radiation defense forces, and his assistant in Moscow on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bolivian prosecutors seek the arrest of former President Morales in sexual abuse case
item.description: LA PAZ, Bolivia (AP) — Prosecutors in Bolivia on Monday sought the arrest of former President Evo Morales in connection to a sexual abuse investigation involving his alleged fathering of a child with a 15-year-old girl.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ukraine and US say some North Korean troops have been killed fighting alongside Russian forces
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine’s military intelligence agency and the Pentagon said Monday that some North Korean troops have been killed during combat against Ukrainian forces in Russia’s Kursk border region. These are the first reported casualties since the U.S.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB and TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Canadian Finance Minister resigns as Trudeau government's popularity flounders
item.description: Canadian Finance Minister Chrystia Freeland announced her resignation from Justin Trudeau's Cabinet on Monday, a powerful blow to the Liberal Prime Minister's hold on the government.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: German Chancellor Olaf Scholz loses no-confidence vote, election called for early next year
item.description: In November, Scholz's government collapsed after the Free Democrat Party quit over the country's debt. An early election to replace Scholz will occur on Feb. 23 following Monday's no-confidence vote.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: FIFA apologizes to Ukraine soccer body for Crimea map error at 2026 World Cup draw
item.description: “While the incident was unintentional, we sincerely regret any concern it may have cause,” FIFA’s chief member association officer wrote in a letter .
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump once stopped Erdogan attacks on US-backed Kurds in Syria, and has opportunity to do it again: Jack Keane
item.description: As the conflict expands between pro-Turkey Islamist forces and the pro-American Kurdish forces, there are growing efforts to rope in Ankara’s offensive.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Reporter's Notebook: Chronicling the Assad regime from death of the father to defeat of the son
item.description: Fox News senior foreign affairs correspondent Greg Palkot documents his years of reporting from Syria in the aftermath of the fall of the regime of Bashar Assad.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Korea lawmakers vote to impeach president over martial law declaration
item.description: South Korean lawmakers on Saturday voted to impeach President Yoon Suk Yeol over his short-lived martial law declaration earlier this month.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Woman arrested for attempting to smuggle 22 pounds of meth wrapped as Christmas gifts in carry-on bag
item.description: A 29-year-old woman was arrested after she allegedly attempted to smuggle methamphetamine wrapped as Christmas presents in her carry-on bag.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Saudi Arabia's plans to host the men's World Cup 2034 will be harmful for the climate, experts say
item.description: As the newly-named host of the 2034 World Cup in men's soccer, Saudi Arabia says it will construct or renovate 15 stadiums, create a futuristic city and expand airports in a massive buildout to accommodate millions of athletes, coaches and spectators.
item.dc:creator: ALEXA ST. JOHN and JENNIFER McDERMOTT, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: France's Macron names centrist ally Bayrou as next prime minister
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron named centrist ally François Bayrou as prime minister, after a historic parliamentary vote ousted the previous government.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US troops to remain in Syria, White House says
item.description: The ouster of Bashar al-Assad has left Biden’s outgoing administration to maneuver through a volatile moment in the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation
